rulings.Case Title: MIA ROBINSON VS. COLE ASHBURY GROUP LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, April 1, 2026, Line 1, DEFENDANT COOKIES CREATIVE CONSULTING & PROMOTIONS, INC.'s MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD. The unopposed motion for withdrawal of attorney of record is denied without prejudice. The motion does not attach the required MC-052 declaration and there is no filed proof of service. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA).

rulings.Case Title: AMIR GUPTA VS. LEAH CULVER ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, April 1, 2026, Line 2, CROSS COMPLAINANT AMIR GUPTA, (DEFENDANT/CROSS-COMPLAINANT FROM CONSOLIDATED CASE #CGC-23-609214), PRAVANA CAPITAL PARTNERS, INC.'s MOTION TO QUASH SUBPOENAS. The unopposed motion to modify the third-party subpoena issued to Wells Fargo Bank, N.A. is granted. Moving parties Amir Gupta and Pravana Capital partners acknowledge some legitimate scope for discovery but complain that the requests are overbroad. Moving parties' opening brief, at pages 4-7, offers some objections and some requests for narrower scope. No opposition was received. The court adopts the proposals at pages 4-7. To implement the proposals, the court orders a first-look procedure: the subpoena officer will provide responsive records to moving parties' counsel, who will redact pursuant to the proposals at pages 4-7 and provide the redacted documents to the subpoenaing party. No sanctions are awarded. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA).

rulings.Case Title: SARAH LEWIS VS. KENNETH RUNYON ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Stay And Quash Plaintiff'S Notice Of Taking Deposition Of Defendant Luis Ernan Magallon
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, April 1, 2026, Line 3, DEFENDANT ENA REALES, LUIS MAGALLON's Motion To Stay And Quash Plaintiff's Notice Of Taking Deposition Of Defendant Luis Ernan Magallon. 1 - The motion to quash the deposition notice of Luis Magallon is granted in part. In the ordinary course, it is good practice for attorneys to abide by the professional courtesy guidelines and schedule depositions in the order they are noticed. But it is not a hard and fast rule. Here, trial is fast approaching and the court concludes it is impractical to employ the guideline. Defendants offer no other reason why plaintiff Sarah Lewis needs to be deposed first. However, counsel's jury summons is good cause to reschedule and Lewis should have accommodated that request. The parties are to meet and confer on new dates and accommodate each other's scheduling issues. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA).

rulings.Case Title: SARAH LEWIS VS. KENNETH RUNYON ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Stay And Quash Plaintiff'S Notice Of Taking Deposition Of Defendant Ena Rosario Reales
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, April 1, 2026, Line 3, DEFENDANT ENA REALES, LUIS MAGALLON's Motion To Stay And Quash Plaintiff's Notice Of Taking Deposition Of Defendant Ena Rosario Reales. 2 - The motion to quash the deposition notice of Ena Reales is granted in part. In the ordinary course, it is good practice for attorneys to abide by the professional courtesy guidelines and schedule depositions in the order they are noticed. But it is not a hard and fast rule. Here, trial is fast approaching and the court concludes it is impractical to employ the guideline. Defendants offer no other reason why plaintiff Sarah Lewis needs to be deposed first. However, counsel's jury summons is good cause to reschedule and Lewis should have accommodated that request. In addition, Reales's need for time to hire new counsel is good cause. The court orders that Reales's deposition shall be noticed after May 1, 2026, to give her time to retain new counsel. Lewis must meet and confer before unilaterally noticing the deposition date. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA).

rulings.Case Title: ALEXANDER XUE VS. ROUND ONE ENTERTAINMENT INC
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, April 1, 2026, Line 7, DEFENDANT ROUND ONE ENTERTAINMENT INC.'s MOTION FOR IMPOSITION OF SANCTIONS. Plaintiff emailed the clerk after business hours on March 27 requesting a non-opposed continuance. As the court has previously explained, continuance requests should be made three court days in advance of the hearing. The court is unavailable on the requested date of April 14 but will continue the motion to April 8, 2026. rulings.Case Title: GARY M WAITE VS. EDWARD M HIGGINBOTHAM
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Quash Second Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, April 1, 2026, Line 4, DEFENDANT EDWARD HIGGINBOTHAM's Motion To Quash Second Amended Complaint. Specially appearing defendant Edward Higginbotham's motion to quash is denied as moot. No proof of service of the summons as to Higginbotham has been filed, and the court rejected plaintiff Gary Waite's request to take his default. For clarity, the court explains that each party in an action is entitled to service of the summons and complaint. Waite's service of the summons and complaint on the Bryant Law Group, LLP, did not effect service on Higginbotham individually because attorneys for Bryant Law Group have not appeared in this action as counsel for Higginbotham and every party to an action has a right to individual service pursuant to the Code unless they agree to some other method of service. Waite must serve Higginbotham as required by the Code in order for the complaint to be at issue as to Higginbotham. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA).
